steps.title: Pin down each author's position
steps.content: Text 1's author says thermodynamic irreversibility is established at the macroscopic level, but current microscopic/quantum laws hold that those processes "should" be reversible — framing this as a puzzle for physicists.

steps.title: See what Text 2 adds
steps.content: Batalhão et al. experimentally confirmed irreversibility at the quantum level. That is direct empirical evidence that the "should be reversible" prediction of current microscopic theory does not hold — meaning the current microscopic understanding must be missing something (a point Paternostro reinforces by noting the experiment doesn't yet pinpoint the cause).

steps.title: Match Text 1's author to a choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'two of the physicists... disagree on the significance': Text 2 shows no such disagreement; this fabricates a conflict not in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — 'current understanding... at a microscopic level must be incomplete': exactly what Text 1's author, who already calls the microscopic prediction puzzling, would conclude from empirical evidence of quantum-level irreversibility. ✔
  • Choice C — 'consistent with the current understanding... at a microscopic level': backwards — the experiment contradicts the microscopic prediction of reversibility. ✗
  • Choice D — 'should not be extrapolated to a general claim': Text 1's author has no stated concern about generalizing lab results; this issue is invented and not what the Text 1 author flags. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
